style_guide.art_style: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending.
style_guide.color_palette: Deep storm blues, grays, electric yellow lightning, fiery orange sunsets, and grassy greens.
style_guide.lighting: Dramatic and dynamic, with strong contrasts during storms, shifting from overcast day to golden sunset to electric light.

characters.name: Mia
characters.description: A girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons. She has wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks. She wears a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers. She always carries a digital weather station device with various sensors.

characters.name: Liam
characters.description: A boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin. He has round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek. He wears a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots. He carries a small blue backpack.

pages.narration: Look up at those clouds! They aren't just puffy white marshmallows today. These are massive, dark supercell thunderstorms, the powerful engines that can make tornadoes. Feel that unstable, sweaty, humid air? It's the perfect recipe for trouble.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style illustration shows dynamic storm clouds and visible texture, featuring Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack, both looking up nervously at massive, swirling dark clouds during an overcast midday. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: Characters look up at large supercell clouds.

pages.narration: Imagine giant invisible winds pushing a football sideways. That's *wind shear*, when wind speeds change directions at different heights, making the air spin like a sideways tube. Then, *KABOOM!* Strong rising air, called an updraft, grabs that spinning tube and tilts it upright.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending, showing Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack. Mia explains a graphic of a horizontal rotating air tube being tilted upwards by an updraft within a turbulent storm, under dramatic lighting. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: Mia explains wind shear using a graphical element.
visual.time_of_day: Overcast day with strong light contrasts

pages.narration: A rotating wall cloud forms below the supercell. Suddenly, a funnel cloud stretches down from it like an elephant’s trunk. It gets closer... and once it touches the ground, it's officially a tornado! Now you get to decide where our exploration goes!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending, showing Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack. They stand a safe distance from a wall cloud where a funnel cloud extends towards the ground, near a sturdy shelter, in dramatic light. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: Safe viewing location near a shelter
visual.action: Tornado touches down in distance; characters decide what to explore.
visual.time_of_day: Overcast day with a distant fiery glow

choice.question: Do you want to learn about measuring a tornado's power or how tornadoes move and behave?

option_a.label: Measuring Power: The Enhanced Fujita Scale
option_a.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style illustration shows a simplified scale graphic and some tornado damage. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: We can't put a tornado on a scale to weigh it, so scientists measure its power by looking at the damage it leaves behind. Dr. Ted Fujita invented a rating system that was later updated to the Enhanced Fujita (EF) Scale. This scale tells us the estimated wind speed and how strong the storm was!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending, showing Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, showing a diagram of the EF scale. She and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack, observe damage to a small structure in a grassy field after a storm. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: Mia shows EF scale diagram while observing damage.

pages.narration: An EF0 is the weakest tornado with winds under 85 mph, maybe knocking over trash cans or peeling off roof shingles. But an EF5 is a monster, with roaring winds *over 200 mph* that can rip strong houses right off their foundations. The EF scale isn't about size, it's about pure destructive force!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending, showing Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack, compare the effects of weak and strong wind speeds. Illustrations of damage from an EF0 (weak) vs an EF5 (monstrous) are visible, under dramatic twilight. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: Characters compare EF0 vs EF5 damage levels.

option_b.label: Movement and Behavior: Tracking Paths and Life Cycles
option_b.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style illustration shows a looping path of a tornado on a map and different funnel stages. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: Tornadoes don't travel in straight lines; they can change direction faster than a distracted chipmunk! They often last only a few minutes, though some powerful ones can rage for over an hour. How far they travel depends completely on the thunderstorm that created them!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending, showing Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, showing a complex, looping track on a screen, and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack. They track a distant tornado's complex path from a safe distance in a field during dynamic light. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: Characters track a tornado's non-linear path.

pages.narration: Every tornado has a life cycle, starting with the funnel touching down and growing to its widest, most destructive phase. As the storm starts to run out of energy, the funnel shrinks and becomes thin and rope-like, then twists away and disappears into the sky!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending, showing Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack, observing different stages of a tornado. Background illustrations show the funnel expanding, then rope-like dissipation, against dramatic cloud formations and lightning. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: Open landscape near powerful storm activity
visual.action: Mia observes and describes different life cycle stages.

pages.narration: Understanding these spectacular, powerful storms is so important for keeping everyone safe in places like Tornado Alley, where they are most common. Remember: when a tornado warning sounds, quickly find a safe place like a basement or an interior room away from windows!
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache style with visible texture and soft blending, showing Mia, a girl, 10 years old, with deep brown skin and short curly black hair worn in two puffs with yellow ribbons, wide dark brown eyes, a small build slightly taller than her brother, and freckles on her nose and cheeks, wearing a bright teal long-sleeved shirt with a yellow thunderbolt icon, dark grey cargo pants, and orange sneakers, always carrying a digital weather station device with various sensors, and Liam, a boy, 7 years old, with deep brown skin and wavy sandy-blonde hair to his chin, round light brown eyes, a small, slight build, and a dimple on his left cheek, wearing a red hoodie with white stripes, blue jeans, and green boots, carrying a small blue backpack, sitting safely together in a sturdy basement shelter. A small weather radio is active, and a gentle light illuminates them. Outside a window high up, a distant storm fades in the golden sunset. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: Characters are safe in a shelter, listening to a weather radio.
